description: ML Algorithm Knowledge Base for the NML Advisor. Maps problem types to algorithms, NML implementation patterns, ML Journey lesson references, and decision heuristics.

problem_types.name: Binary Classification
problem_types.description: Predict one of two categories (yes/no, spam/ham, churn/stay, fraud/legit).

algorithms.name: Logistic Regression
algorithms.when: Small dataset (<10k rows), linearly separable features, need interpretability, baseline model.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD + SIGM → probability. Threshold at 0.5.

algorithms.name: 2-Layer MLP
algorithms.when: Medium dataset (1k–100k), non-linear relationships, feature interactions matter.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD + RELU (hidden) → MMUL + MADD + SIGM (output). Train with TRAIN RU [@data @labels] or manual BKWD+WUPD.
algorithms.lesson_ref: Lesson 06 — Classification Basics, Lesson 13 — Neural Networks

algorithms.name: MLP with BN + Dropout
algorithms.when: Small-to-medium dataset where overfitting is a risk, need regularization.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD + BN + RELU + DROP per hidden layer → SIGM output.

algorithms.name: SVM (Linear)
algorithms.when: High-dimensional sparse features (text), clean decision boundary, <50k samples.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD → decision value. Positive = class 1, negative = class 0.

algorithms.name: Decision Tree
algorithms.when: Need full interpretability (explain every decision), categorical features, regulatory requirements.
algorithms.nml_pattern: CMPF + JMPT/JMPF branching → LEAF terminal values.

algorithms.name: Naive Bayes
algorithms.when: Text classification, very fast training needed, features are roughly independent.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Log-probability accumulation: EMUL + SADD + SOFT.

problem_types.name: Multi-Class Classification
problem_types.description: Predict one of 3+ categories (species, product type, sentiment level, digit recognition).

algorithms.name: Softmax Classifier
algorithms.when: Baseline multi-class, linearly separable features.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD + SOFT → K probabilities summing to 1.0.

algorithms.name: Multi-Layer MLP + Softmax
algorithms.when: Non-linear class boundaries, medium dataset, need class probabilities.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD + RELU (hidden layers) → MMUL + MADD + SOFT (output). Use LOSS #2 (cross-entropy).

algorithms.name: CNN Image Classifier
algorithms.when: Image input, spatial features matter, pattern recognition.
algorithms.nml_pattern: CONV + POOL + RELU → flatten → MMUL + SOFT.

problem_types.name: Regression
problem_types.description: Predict a continuous numeric value (price, temperature, score, quantity).

algorithms.name: Linear Regression
algorithms.when: Simple relationships, few features, need interpretability, baseline.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD → predicted value. Loss: LOSS #0 (MSE).

algorithms.name: Multiple Regression
algorithms.when: Multiple input features, linear relationships.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL (features × weights) + MADD (bias) → prediction.

algorithms.name: Neural Network Regression
algorithms.when: Non-linear relationships, feature interactions, medium-to-large dataset.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL + MADD + RELU (hidden) → MMUL + MADD (linear output). Train with TRAIN RU [@data @labels], LOSS #0 (MSE).

algorithms.name: Decision Tree Regression
algorithms.when: Non-linear, need interpretability, categorical features, price brackets.
algorithms.nml_pattern: CMPF + JMPT/JMPF branching → LEAF terminal values.

algorithms.name: Gradient Boosted Ensemble
algorithms.when: Tabular data, need best accuracy, willing to trade interpretability.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Multiple CMPF/LEAF trees → weighted MADD combination → VOTE consensus.

problem_types.name: Clustering
problem_types.description: Group similar items without labels. Customer segments, document topics, anomaly groups.

algorithms.name: K-Means Distance
algorithms.when: Known number of clusters, numeric features, roughly spherical clusters.
algorithms.nml_pattern: MSUB (point - centroid) → EMUL (square) → MMUL with ones (sum) → compare distances.

problem_types.name: Anomaly Detection
problem_types.description: Find outliers, unusual patterns, defects, fraud. Often unsupervised or semi-supervised.

algorithms.name: Autoencoder
algorithms.when: High-dimensional data, learn normal patterns, reconstruction error = anomaly score.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Encoder: MMUL + MADD + RELU → bottleneck. Decoder: MMUL + MADD + SIGM → reconstruction. Error: MSUB + EMUL + sum.
algorithms.lesson_ref: Lesson 03 — Unsupervised Learning, Lesson 13 — Neural Networks

algorithms.name: Threshold Classifier
algorithms.when: Single score/feature, simple rule-based detection, sensor threshold.
algorithms.nml_pattern: CMPR (score > threshold) → WHER (flag anomalies).

algorithms.name: Neural Network Anomaly Detector
algorithms.when: Complex feature interactions, labeled normal/anomaly data available.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Multi-layer: MMUL + MADD + RELU → SIGM output. Score > 0.5 = anomaly.

problem_types.name: Dimensionality Reduction
problem_types.description: Compress high-dimensional data to fewer dimensions for visualization or preprocessing.

algorithms.name: Autoencoder Compression
algorithms.when: Non-linear compression, learn meaningful low-dimensional representation.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Encoder: MMUL + MADD + RELU → bottleneck (low-dim). ST bottleneck as embedding.

algorithms.name: Linear Projection
algorithms.when: Simple linear compression, known projection matrix (PCA-like).
algorithms.nml_pattern: MMUL input × projection_matrix → low-dim output.

problem_types.name: Ensemble Methods
problem_types.description: Combine multiple models for better accuracy and robustness.

algorithms.name: Voting Ensemble
algorithms.when: Multiple diverse models available, want robustness, reduce variance.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Run N models independently → collect predictions → VOTE (median/mean).

algorithms.name: Gradient Boosting
algorithms.when: Tabular data, need state-of-the-art accuracy, sequential correction of errors.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Tree1 → residual → Tree2 → residual → Tree3 → weighted sum.

problem_types.name: Computer Vision
problem_types.description: Process images: classification, object detection, edge detection, segmentation.

algorithms.name: CNN Pipeline
algorithms.when: Image classification, feature extraction from 2D data.
algorithms.nml_pattern: PADZ → CONV + POOL + RELU (feature extraction) → RSHP (flatten) → MMUL + SOFT (classify).

algorithms.name: Edge Detection
algorithms.when: Simple feature extraction, preprocessing for downstream tasks.
algorithms.nml_pattern: CONV with edge kernel (Sobel/Laplacian) → POOL.

problem_types.name: NLP / Transformers
problem_types.description: Process text: sentiment analysis, text generation, translation, summarization.

algorithms.name: Transformer Block
algorithms.when: Sequential/text data, need attention over full context, language understanding.
algorithms.nml_pattern: EMBD → positional add → ATTN (Q,K,V) → NORM → MMUL + GELU → NORM → output.

algorithms.name: Sentiment Classifier
algorithms.when: Classify text into positive/negative/neutral. Simpler than full transformer for short text.
algorithms.nml_pattern: EMBD → mean pool → MMUL + MADD + SOFT (classify).

problem_types.name: Time Series / Sequential
problem_types.description: Predict future values from historical sequences. Stock prices, sensor readings, demand forecasting.

algorithms.name: Sliding Window MLP
algorithms.when: Fixed-length history window, tabular time features.
algorithms.nml_pattern: Concatenate last N values → MMUL + MADD + RELU → MMUL + MADD → prediction.

algorithms.name: Transformer Time Series
algorithms.when: Long-range dependencies, multiple channels, attention over time steps.
algorithms.nml_pattern: EMBD positions → ATTN over time steps → NORM → linear projection.

problem_types.name: Signal Processing
problem_types.description: Frequency analysis, noise filtering, audio processing.

algorithms.name: FFT + Frequency Filter
algorithms.when: Remove noise at specific frequencies, analyze spectral content.
algorithms.nml_pattern: FFT → FILT (frequency domain filter) → inverse FFT.

problem_types.name: Training Optimization
problem_types.description: Improve model training: regularization, normalization, learning rate control.

algorithms.name: Batch Normalization
algorithms.when: Deep networks, training instability, internal covariate shift.
algorithms.nml_pattern: BN Rd Rs Rgamma Rbeta after each linear layer, before activation.

algorithms.name: Dropout
algorithms.when: Overfitting on small dataset, co-adaptation of neurons.
algorithms.nml_pattern: DROP Rd Rs #rate after activation. Use #0.0 at inference.

algorithms.name: Weight Decay (L2)
algorithms.when: Prevent large weights, generalization.
algorithms.nml_pattern: WDECAY Rw #lambda before each WUPD step.

decision_heuristics.recommendation: Use simpler models (logistic regression, decision tree, Naive Bayes). Add dropout/BN if using neural networks.
decision_heuristics.avoid: Deep networks without regularization — they will overfit.

decision_heuristics.recommendation: Neural networks can shine. Consider MLP for tabular, CNN for images, Transformer for sequences.

decision_heuristics.condition: Need to explain predictions to stakeholders
decision_heuristics.recommendation: Decision trees, logistic regression, or ensemble with SHAP values.
decision_heuristics.avoid: Deep neural networks — they are black boxes.

decision_heuristics.recommendation: Decision trees, Naive Bayes, or gradient boosting handle categorical data naturally.
decision_heuristics.avoid: Raw neural networks — need careful encoding first.

decision_heuristics.condition: Input is images or spatial data
decision_heuristics.recommendation: CNN with CONV + POOL layers. Use PADZ for border handling.
Transformer with EMBD + ATTN + NORM. Or simple bag-of-words + MLP for short text classification.
decision_heuristics.avoid: CNN (unless 1D-conv for text), decision trees (can't handle variable-length input).

decision_heuristics.recommendation: Unsupervised: K-Means clustering, autoencoder for anomaly detection, dimensionality reduction.
Small models: logistic regression, small MLP, decision tree. NML programs are lightweight and execute efficiently.
decision_heuristics.avoid: Large ensembles or deep transformers in the hot path.

decision_heuristics.condition: Training on edge device / limited memory
decision_heuristics.recommendation: TRAIN with small architecture (1-2 layers). Use BN for training stability. Keep tensor shapes small.
decision_heuristics.avoid: Large batch sizes, deep networks, attention layers.

decision_heuristics.recommendation: Train locally on each worker's data with TRAIN, use VOTE consensus for model agreement.
decision_heuristics.avoid: Centralized data pooling — defeats the purpose of distributed execution.

supervised_training.config_driven: TRAIN RU [@data @labels] — config-driven training. RV=architecture [n_layers, h1, act1, h2, act2, ...], RU=config [epochs, lr, optimizer(0=SGD,1=Adam), print_every, patience, min_delta]. Built-in early stopping, Adam, logging. Use INFER Rd Rs for forward-only after training.
supervised_training.manual_loop: LOOP #N → forward → LOSS → BKWD → WUPD → ENDP

inference_only.pattern: LD weights → forward pass → ST output. No TRAIN/BKWD/WUPD.
inference_only.infer_opcode: INFER Rd Rs — config-driven forward pass only, no weight update.

regularization.batch_norm: BN Rd Rs [Rgamma] [Rbeta] — after linear, before activation.
regularization.dropout: DROP Rd Rs #rate — after activation. #0.0 disables for inference.
regularization.weight_decay: WDECAY Rw #lambda — before WUPD in training loop.

data_shapes.rule: NML tensors are 2D: [rows, cols]. Images use RSHP for 4D [N,C,H,W].
data_shapes.labels: @training_labels shape=N,O where O=output_dim (1 for binary, K for multi-class)

data_guide.overview: Every NML program needs a .nml.data file that defines named tensors. Each tensor has a real-world meaning — make this explicit.

input_data.purpose: The raw data you want to process or learn from.
examples: @input shape=1,4 — one sample with 4 features (e.g., temperature, pressure, vibration, speed)
@training_data shape=100,6 — 100 transactions, each with 6 features (amount, time, merchant_category, distance, frequency, is_international)
input_data.notes: Rows = samples/observations. Columns = features/measurements. Normalize numeric features to 0-1 range.

labels.purpose: The correct answer for supervised training — what you want the model to learn to predict.
labels.notes: Must have same number of rows as training_data. Not needed for unsupervised (autoencoder, k-means).

weights.purpose: Learnable parameters that the model adjusts during training. Initialize small random or zeros.
examples: @w1 shape=6,8 — connects 6 input features to 8 hidden neurons
@b1 shape=1,8 — one bias per hidden neuron (initialize to 0.0)
weights.naming: Convention: @w1/@b1 for layer 1, @w2/@b2 for layer 2, etc. Shape: [input_dim, output_dim] for weights, [1, output_dim] for biases.
weights.notes: TRAIN auto-creates weights from RV architecture descriptor — you only need to provide data and labels.

inference_input.purpose: A new, unseen sample to run through the trained model for prediction.
examples: @new_transaction shape=1,6 — one new transaction to score for fraud
@test_image shape=1,784 — one 28x28 image flattened to a row
inference_input.notes: Must have same number of columns as training features. Only 1 row for single prediction.

reconstruction_target.purpose: For autoencoders: the target output is the input itself (learn to reconstruct).
examples: Labels = training_data. Reconstruction error = anomaly score.

data_guide.train_shortcut: When using TRAIN config-driven training: RV = architecture descriptor [n_layers, h1, act1, h2, act2, ...], RU = config [epochs, lr, optimizer, print_every, patience, min_delta], R0 = input data, R9 = labels. TRAIN RU [@data @labels] creates and manages all intermediate weights automatically. Use INFER Rd Rs for forward-only prediction after training.

01.teaches: ML vs programming, terminology, first model concept

03.teaches: K-Means, autoencoders, anomaly detection without labels

05.teaches: Linear regression, gradient descent, Python + NML

08.teaches: Bayes theorem, text classification, log-probability trick

09.teaches: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1, overfitting, cross-validation

10.teaches: L1/L2, dropout, batch norm, Adam, learning rate

12.teaches: XGBoost, boosting vs bagging, hyperparameter tuning

14.teaches: Convolutions, pooling, feature maps, transfer learning

17.teaches: Complete workflow: data → train → evaluate → deploy
